questions.text: Has the provider defined a governance policy for logging and traceability?
Are roles and responsibilities for logging oversight clearly assigned (who maintains, audits, and reviews logs)?
Are access rules and authorization levels documented and aligned with security and data protection requirements?
Has a logging plan been approved by the responsible compliance or risk officer?
Are logging requirements integrated into the system architecture from the design phase (including interfaces and data flows)?
Are procedures in place to ensure logs remain comprehensive and tamper-resistant as the system evolves or updates?
Has the provider documented which events are logged (e.g., performance, cybersecurity, modifications, anomalies)?
Does the provider maintain an information model for log fields (event type, timestamp, user ID, action, outcome, etc.)?
Is logging implemented across all lifecycle stages (development, training, validation, testing, deployment)?
Are logs technically protected (e.g., integrity checks, secure storage, controlled access)?
Are privacy and data protection considerations integrated into the logging design (especially for sensitive data)?
Are justification and rationale for logged events documented in the technical file, especially for safety- or rights-relevant events?
Has the logging mechanism been tested for accuracy, completeness, and resilience against tampering?
Are procedures in place to verify log accessibility by authorized personnel under controlled conditions?
Are logs reviewed during validation to confirm they support incident reconstruction and risk assessment?
Are log analysis tools documented and validated for usability and interpretability?
Have cybersecurity event logging and alert mechanisms been tested for reliability?
Is there a process for testing log retention and retrieval?
Do validation records demonstrate that logging supports monitoring, explainability, and traceability requirements?
Is there a procedure for periodic review of logs to detect anomalies, errors, or security events?
Are designated investigators or auditors able to access logs promptly in case of significant incidents?
Are logs integrated with the risk management and incident reporting workflows?
Are changes to system configurations, models, or parameters automatically logged?
Is user feedback captured in logs to support ongoing risk monitoring?
Are logging mechanisms updated and revalidated when system modifications occur?
Are update and rollback procedures logged to maintain traceability of software or model versions?
Are procedures in place to ensure continuity of logs across version updates or infrastructure migrations?
Are lessons learned from incident investigations used to refine logging practices?
